title: cleomella plocasperma
intro: Cleomella plocasperma, also known as Mexican poppy or Mexican prickle poppy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Cleomaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Cleomella plocasperma include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antispasmodic and antacid properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, teas, and tinctures, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin irritations and wounds.
